sn5.8:1.2: Then, early in the morning, Sīsupacālā the nun adjusted her robes and, taking her bowl & outer robe, went into Sāvatthī for alms.
sn5.8:1.3: When she had gone for alms in Sāvatthī and had returned from her alms round, after her meal she went to the Grove of the Blind to spend the day.
sn5.8:1.4: Having gone deep into the Grove of the Blind, she sat down at the foot of a tree for the day’s abiding. Then Māra the Evil One approached her & said,
sn5.8:1.5: “Whose philosophy do you approve of, nun?”
sn5.8:1.6: “I don’t approve of anyone’s philosophy, my friend.”
sn5.8:2.1: Māra: “For whose sake have you shaved your head?
sn5.8:2.3: but don’t approve of a philosophy,
sn5.8:2.4: so why are you wandering here confused?”
sn5.8:3.3: I don’t approve of their teaching.
sn5.8:3.4: They’re not adept in the Dhamma.
sn5.8:4.1: But there is the Awakened One,
sn5.8:5.3: all-seeing, reaching the end of all kamma—
sn5.8:5.4: with the ending of acquisitions, released.
sn5.8:5.5: He, that Blessed One, is my teacher.
sn5.8:5.6: It’s in his Dhamma that I delight.”
sn5.8:6.1: Then Māra the Evil One—sad & dejected at realizing, “Sīsupacālā the nun knows me”—vanished right there.
